steps.title: Understand the parabola's sign pattern
steps.content:
The function $f(x) = (x - a)(x - b)$ is an upward-opening parabola with roots at $x = a$ and $x = b$. For such a parabola:
  • $f(x) < 0$ when $x$ is between the two roots
  • $f(x) > 0$ when $x$ is outside both roots
The given conditions show the pattern: $f(15) > 0$, $f(18) < 0$, $f(21) > 0$ — that is, positive, negative, positive.

steps.title: Locate the roots from sign changes
steps.content:
Since $f$ changes from positive (at $x=15$) to negative (at $x=18$), there must be a root between 15 and 18.
Since $f$ changes from negative (at $x=18$) to positive (at $x=21$), there must be a root between 18 and 21.
Therefore, the two roots lie in the intervals $(15, 18)$ and $(18, 21)$ respectively.

steps.title: Determine integer values for the roots
steps.content:
Since $a$ and $b$ are integers:
  • The root in $(15, 18)$ must be one of {16, 17}
  • The root in $(18, 21)$ must be one of {19, 20}
This gives four possible pairs: $(16, 19)$, $(16, 20)$, $(17, 19)$, and $(17, 20)$.

steps.title: Calculate sums and match to choices
steps.content:
Computing $a + b$ for each pair:
  • $16 + 19 = 35$
  • $16 + 20 = 36$
  • $17 + 19 = 36$
  • $17 + 20 = 37$
Among the answer choices (18, 20, 33, 35), only 35 appears. That matches choice D — D is correct.
